title: sphaeromorphaea ephemera
intro: Sphaeromorphaea ephemera, also known as the ephemeral daisy, is a rare and short-lived flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the daisy family and is known for its white or pink flowers and leaves that resemble those of the lettuce plant. The medicinal properties of Sphaeromorphaea ephemera include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to provide relief from digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, and also have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects.
